title: Lindernia hyssopioides
taxonomy: Lindernia hyssopioides is classified within the family Linderniaceae, which is a small family of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants that also includes mint, lavender, and snapdragons. At the kingdom level, Lindernia hyssopioides is a member of the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants. The species is also known to be part of the subfamily Lindernioideae and the genus Lindernia. This classification provides a way to understand the evolutionary relationships among different species of plants.

intro: Lindernia hyssopioides, also known as False Hyssop or Water Purlene, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Linderniaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lindernia hyssopioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
